config-cli/src/test/java/de/adorsys/keyc.general_idea: Update lifecycle annotations from JUnit 4 to JUnit 5.
config-cli/src/test/java/de/adorsys/keyc.why_exists: JUnit 5 does not recognize @Before and @After; tests will fail to execute lifecycle methods or fail to compile if the annotations are not replaced with @BeforeEach and @AfterEach.

config-cli/src/test/java/de/adorsys/keyc.general_idea: Replace JUnit 4 Assert.assertThat with Hamcrest MatcherAssert.assertThat.
config-cli/src/test/java/de/adorsys/keyc.why_exists: org.junit.Assert is removed in JUnit 5; using it causes compilation errors. Hamcrest's MatcherAssert is the standard replacement for assertThat in JUnit 5 environments.

config-cli/src/test/java/de/adorsys/keyc.general_idea: Replace JUnit 4 Assert.assertThat with Hamcrest MatcherAssert.assertThat.
config-cli/src/test/java/de/adorsys/keyc.why_exists: org.junit.Assert is removed in JUnit 5; using it causes compilation errors. Hamcrest's MatcherAssert is the standard replacement for assertThat in JUnit 5 contexts.

config-cli/src/test/java/de/adorsys/keyc.general_idea: Migrate test annotations and assertions from JUnit 4 to JUnit 5 (Jupiter) to align with Spring Boot 3.x requirements.
config-cli/src/test/java/de/adorsys/keyc.why_exists: JUnit 4 classes (org.junit.Test, org.junit.Assert) are not part of the default classpath in Spring Boot 3.x projects configured for JUnit 5, leading to ClassNotFoundException or compilation errors if dependencies are not explicitly added and configured.

config-cli/src/test/java/de/adorsys/keyc.general_idea: Replace legacy List.get(0) calls with the new List.getFirst() method introduced in Java 21.
config-cli/src/test/java/de/adorsys/keyc.why_exists: List.getFirst() is a new API in Java 21; while get(0) still compiles, the intent is to modernize code to use the safer, more expressive API available in the target JDK.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update Maven compiler source and target properties from Java 11 to Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project compiles against Java 11 APIs and bytecode, failing to utilize Java 21 features or potentially causing runtime issues if Java 21-specific code is introduced.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Boot version from 2.2.4.RELEASE to 3.3.13.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.x requires Java 17+ and uses Jakarta EE namespaces (jakarta.* instead of javax.*). Keeping the old version would cause compilation errors due to missing javax packages or classpath conflicts with Java 21.
